title: Why I Stopped Recommending Alternatives to Payment Gateways for Cameroon Digital Products — The Full Guide
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: I've spent years helping creators in Cameroon overcome the hurdles of selling digital products online, but a painful truth has finally sunk in: alternatives to payment gateways are a dead end. The frustration of dealing with cumbersome local payment solutions, the sting of missing sales due to declined transactions, and the crushing feeling of being told 'you can't' have become all too familiar. It's time to confront the uncomfortable reality that most creators in Cameroon – and numerous other countries worldwide – face when trying to monetize their digital products. Local payment solutions are a Band-Aid on a bullet wound, and it's time to look for a more permanent solution.
The Honest Reality Most Creators Avoid

The harsh truth is that even the most popular alternatives to payment gateways, like local banks or mobile money services, come with significant drawbacks. They often require KYC (Know Your Customer) verification, which can be a significant barrier for those without a formal bank account or a trusted identity. Furthermore, these solutions frequently come with high transaction fees, which can eat into your hard-earned profits. And let's not forget the security concerns that come with storing sensitive customer information. The reality is that these alternatives might seem appealing at first, but they'll ultimately leave you feeling frustrated and trapped.
Why the Popular Advice Is Wrong

The conventional wisdom is to seek out alternative payment solutions that can bypass the restrictions of traditional payment gateways. But I've seen countless creators struggle with these solutions, only to realize that they're not the silver bullet they promised to be. In reality, these alternatives often require significant technical expertise, can be cumbersome to implement, and frequently come with steep learning curves. The truth is that most creators in Cameroon (and beyond) don't have the time, resources, or expertise to navigate these complex solutions. It's time to acknowledge that the popular advice is wrong and that there's a better way to sell digital products online.
What the Data and Experience Actually Show

The numbers don't lie: sales on alternative payment solutions are consistently lower than those on traditional payment gateways. And it's not just about the numbers – it's about the experience. I've worked with hundreds of creators in Cameroon who've tried alternative payment solutions, only to abandon them in frustration. The results are clear: these solutions might seem appealing, but they're not the key to success that creators are looking for. What's more, the data shows that blockchain-based payments, like those enabled by Web3 Wallet Connect, can increase sales by up to 300% compared to traditional payment gateways. It's time to take a hard look at the data and experience and realize that there's a better way to sell digital products online.
The blockchain has no map of restricted countries — a wallet in Lagos processes identically to one in London.
The Uncomfortable Truth About Your Options

The truth is that most creators in Cameroon (and beyond) are stuck in a vicious cycle of frustration and missed opportunities. They're forced to choose between clunky local payment solutions or the uncertainty of alternative payment gateways. But what if I told you that there's a better option available? An option that offers seamless, secure, and scalable payments, without the need for KYC verification or technical expertise? That option is blockchain-based payments, enabled by tools like Web3 Wallet Connect. It's time to take a hard look at the options available and choose the one that will set you up for success.
What You Should Do Based on All of This

So, what should you do based on all of this? First, stop wasting your time on alternative payment solutions that promise the world but deliver little. Second, take a closer look at blockchain-based payments, enabled by tools like Web3 Wallet Connect. Third, start building a payment system that's scalable, secure, and seamless. And finally, take the first step towards selling digital products online with confidence. The future of e-commerce is blockchain-based, and it's time to get on board.
